author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. x. 28, ‘Be not afraid of them that kill the body.’ Delivered on a Festival of Martyrs.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. x. 28, ‘Be not afraid of them that kill the body.’ Delivered on a Festival of Martyrs.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Divine oracles which have just been read teach us in fearing not to fear, and in not fearing to fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye observed when the Holy Gospel was being read, that our Lord God before He died for us, would have us to be firm; and this by admonishing us “not” to fear, and withal to fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he said, “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See where He advised us not to fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See now where He advised us to fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But,” saith he, “fear Him who hath power to destroy both body and soul in hell.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us fear therefore, that we may not fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear seems to be allied to cowardice: seems to be the character of the weak, not the strong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But see what saith the Scripture, “The fear of the Lord is the hope of strength.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then fear, that we may not fear; that is, let us fear prudently, that we may not fear vainly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The holy Martyrs on the occasion of whose solemnity this lesson was read out of the Gospel, in fearing, feared not; because in fearing God, they did not regard men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what need a man fear from man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is that whereby one man should cause another fear, since both of them are men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One threatens and says, “I will kill thee;” and does not fear, lest after his threat he die before he have fulfilled it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will kill thee,” he says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who says it, and to whom?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I hear two men, the one threatening, and the other alarmed: of whom the one is powerful, and the other weak, yet both are mortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then does he so stretch out himself, he, in honour, a somewhat more inflated power, in body, equal weakness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him securely threaten death who does not fear death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he fear that whereby he causes fear; let him think of himself, and compare himself with him whom he is threatening.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him see in him whom he threateneth a likeness of condition, and so together with him let him seek like pity from the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he is but a man, and he threatens another man, a creature, another creature; only the one puffed up under his Creator’s eye, and the other fleeing for refuge to the same Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the stout Martyr then, as he stands a man before another man, say; “I do not fear, because I fear.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst not do what thou art threatening, unless He will; but what He threateneth, none can hinder Him from doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then again, what dost thou threaten, and what canst thou do, if thou art permitted?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy violence extends but to the flesh, the soul is safe from thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst not kill what thou dost not see: visible thyself, thou threatenest that which is visible in me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we have both an invisible Creator, whom we ought both to fear; who of that which was both visible and invisible created man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He made Him visible out of the earth, and with His Breath He breathed into Him an invisible Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the invisible substance, that is, the soul, which has raised from the earth the earth as it lay, does not fear, when thou assaultest the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst strike the habitation, but canst thou strike him who dwells there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the chain is broken, he escapes who before was bound, and he will now be crowned in secret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then dost thou threaten me, who canst do nothing to my soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Through the desert of that to which thou canst do nothing, will that to which thy power extends rise again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For through the soul’s desert, will the flesh also rise again; and will be restored to its inhabitant, now no more to fail, but to endure for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold (I am using the words of a Martyr), behold, I say, not even on account of my body do I fear thy threats.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My body indeed is subject to thy power; but even the hairs of my head are numbered by my Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why should I fear lest I lose my body, who cannot even lose a hair?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How shall he not have a care of my body, to whom my meanest things are so well known?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This body which may be wounded and slain will for a time be ashes, but it will be for ever immortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to whom shall this be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom shall the body be restored for life eternal, even though it have been slain, destroyed, and scattered to the winds? to whom shall it be so restored?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To him who has not been afraid to lay down his own life, since he does not fear, lest his body should be slain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, Brethren, the soul is said to be immortal, and immortal it is according to a certain manner of its own: for it is a kind of life which is able to give life to the body by its presence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by the soul doth the body live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This life cannot die, and therefore is the soul immortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then said I according to a certain manner of its own?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because there is a true immortality, an immortality which is an entire unchangeableness; of which the Apostle saith, speaking of God, “Who only hath immortality, dwelling in that light which no man may approach unto, whom no man hath seen, nor can see, to whom be honour and glory for ever and ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then God only hath immortality, the soul must needs be mortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then why it was that I said that the soul is immortal after a certain manner of its own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in fact it may also die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Understand this, Beloved, and there will remain no difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I venture to say then that the soul can die, can be slain also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet it is undoubtedly immortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, I venture to say, it is at once immortal, and it may be slain; and therefore I said that there is a kind of immortality, an entire unchangeableness, that is, which God Only hath, of whom it is said, “Who Only hath immortality;” for if the soul cannot be slain, how did the Lord Himself say, when He would make us fear, “Fear Him who hath power to slay both body and soul in hell”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hitherto I have confirmed, not solved, the difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have proved that the soul can be slain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel cannot be gainsaid but by the ungodly soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, something occurs to me here, and comes into my mind to speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Life cannot be gainsaid, but by a dead soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel is life, impiety and infidelity are the death of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, it can die, and yet it is immortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then is it immortal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because there is always a sort of life which is never extinguished in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how does it die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not in ceasing to be life, but by losing its life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the soul is both life to something else, and it has its own proper life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider the order of the creatures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The soul is the life of the body: God is the life of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the life, that is the soul, is present with the body, that the body die not; so ought the life of the soul, that is God, to be with it that the soul die not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How does the body die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the soul’s leaving it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say, by the soul’s leaving it the body dies; and it lies along a mere carcass, what was a little before a desirable, now a contemptible, object.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are in it still its several members, the eyes, and ears; but these are but the windows of the house, its inhabitant is gone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who bewail the dead, cry in vain at the windows of the house; there is none within to hear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many things does the fond affection of the mourner give utterance to, how many enumerate and call to mind; and with what a madness of sorrow, so to say, does he speak, as with one who was sensible of what was doing, when he is really speaking with one who is no longer there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He recounts his good qualities, and the tokens of his goodness towards himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was thou that didst give me this; and did this and that for me; it was thou who didst thus and thus dearly love me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou wouldest only consider and understand, and restrain the madness of thy grief, he who once loved thee, is gone; in vain does the house receive thy knockings, in which thou canst not find a dweller.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us return to the subject I was speaking of a little while since.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The body is dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because its life, that is the soul, is gone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, the body is alive, and the man is impious, unbelieving, hard of belief, incorrigible; in this case whilst the body is alive, the soul by which the body lives is dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the soul is so excellent a thing, that it has power even though dead to give life to the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So excellent a thing, I say, is the soul, so excellent a creature, that even though dead itself, it has power to quicken the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the soul of the impious, unbelieving, unregulated man is dead, and yet by it though dead the body lives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore is it in the body; it sets on the hands to work, and the feet to walk; it directs the eye to see, it disposes the ears to hear, it discriminates tastes, avoids pains, seeks after pleasures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All these are tokens of the life of the body; but they are from the presence of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I were to ask a body whether it were alive; it would answer me, You see me walking, you see me working, you hear me talking, you perceive that I have certain aims and aversions, and do you not understand that the body is alive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By these works then of the soul which is placed within, I understand that the body is alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask the soul also whether it is alive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It also has its proper works, by which it manifests its life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The feet walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I understand by this that the body lives, but by the presence of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask now, does the soul live?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These feet walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: (To speak only of this one movement.)

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am questioning both body and soul, as touching their life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The feet walk, I understand that the body lives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whither do they walk?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To adultery, it is said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then is the soul dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so hath unerring Scripture said, “The widow who liveth in pleasure is dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now since the difference is great between “pleasure” and adultery, how can the soul which is said to be dead in pleasure, live in adultery?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is surely dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is dead even though it be not in this case.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I hear a man speaking; the body then lives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the tongue could not move itself in the mouth, and by its several motions give utterance to articulate sounds, were there not an inhabitant within; and a musician as it were to this instrument, to make use of his tongue.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I understand it perfectly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus the body speaks; the body then lives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I ask, is the soul alive also?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the body speaks, and so is alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what does it speak?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As I said concerning the feet; they walk, and so the body is alive, and I then asked, whither do they walk? that I might understand whether the soul was alive also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So also when I hear a man speak, I understand that the body is alive; I ask what does he speak, that I may know whether the soul is alive also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He speaks a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If so, then is the soul dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How do we prove this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us ask the truth itself, which saith, “The mouth that lieth, slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask, why is the soul dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask as I did just now, why is the body dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the soul, its life, was gone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is the soul dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because God, its life, hath forsaken it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After this brief examination then, know and hold for certain that the body is dead without the soul, and that the soul is dead without God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every man without God hath a dead soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost bewail the dead: bewail the sinner rather, bewail rather the ungodly man, bewail the unbeliever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is written, “The mourning for the dead is seven days; for a fool and an ungodly man all the days of his life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! are there no bowels of Christian compassion in thee; that thou mournest for a body from which the soul is gone, and mournest not for the soul, from which God is departed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the Martyr remembering this make answer to him that threatens him, “Why dost thou force me to deny Christ?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou then force me to deny the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if I will not, what wilt thou do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt assault my body, that my soul shall depart from it; but this same soul of mine has its body only for the soul’s sake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not so foolish or unwise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wouldest wound my body; but wouldest thou, that through fear lest thou shouldest wound my body, and my soul should depart from it, I should wound mine own soul, and my God should depart from it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear not then, O Martyr, the sword of thy executioner; fear only thine own tongue, lest thou do execution upon thine own self, and slay, not thy body, but thy soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear for thy soul, lest it die in hell-fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore said the Lord, “Who hath power to slay both body and soul in hell-fire.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How? when the ungodly shall be cast into hell-fire, will his body and his soul burn there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Everlasting punishment will be the death of the body; the absence of God will be the death of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou know what the death of the soul is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Understand the Prophet who saith, “Let the ungodly be taken away, that he may not see the glory of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the soul then fear its proper death, and not fear the death of its body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because if it fear its own death, and so live in its God, by not offending and thrusting Him away from him, it will be found worthy to receive its body again at the end; not unto everlasting punishment, as the ungodly, but unto life eternal, as the righteous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By fearing this death, and loving that life, did the Martyrs, in hope of the promises of God, and in contempt of the threats of persecutors, attain themselves to be crowned with God, and have left to us the celebration of these solemnities.
